title: Genmab — Hematology — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in Genmab Hematology — Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-24 (retrospective) Quarter covered: October – December 2025 Data sources: Genmab FY2025 Annual Report (Feb 17, 2026), AbbVie FY2025/Q4 earnings (Feb 4, 2026), FDA approval announcement (Nov 18, 2025)

1. TL;DR

  1. FDA approved EPKINLY + R2 for 2L+ R/R Follicular Lymphoma (Nov 18, 2025) — the third indication for Epkinly and the first bispecific combination therapy approved in lymphoma. Simultaneously converted the FL monotherapy accelerated approval to full approval.
  2. NCCN granted Category 1 Preferred status for epcoritamab + R2 in 2L FL — the fastest possible guideline uptake, signaling immediate clinical practice adoption.
  3. Genmab FY2025 EPKINLY global net sales: $468M (+67% YoY) — acceleration driven by the FL combo approval in late Q4. Genmab net product sales (US + Japan): $379M (+50%).
  4. Genmab 2026 guidance: $4.1-4.4B total revenue — with EPKINLY and Tivdak as the net product sales growth drivers.
  5. Genmab stock dropped 5% on FY2025 results — market viewed mixed EPKINLY data (strong FL, weak DLBCL-1 OS) as concerning despite the top-line revenue beat.

2. Data Events That Matter

FDA Approves EPKINLY + R2 in R/R FL (Nov 18, 2025)

  • What happened: FDA approved epcoritamab + rituximab + lenalidomide (R2) for adult patients with R/R FL after at least one prior line of systemic therapy, based on EPCORE FL-1 Phase 3 data. Simultaneously, the accelerated approval for FL monotherapy (3L+) was converted to full approval.
  • What it means: This is the most significant commercial catalyst for Epkinly since the original DLBCL launch. The FL combo expands the addressable population significantly — R/R FL after 1L is a much larger market than 3L+ DLBCL. The NCCN Category 1 Preferred designation means oncologists have immediate guideline support for adoption. The fixed-duration dosing (12 cycles) in the combo setting also addresses physician concerns about treat-to-progression bispecific therapy.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The FL combo approval changes the growth trajectory. With NCCN Cat 1 Preferred, this should see faster adoption than the original DLBCL launch. We need to be measuring FL-specific adoption metrics — our current tracker is DLBCL-only."

Genmab FY2025: EPKINLY at $468M (+67%)

  • What happened: Full-year 2025 EPKINLY global net sales reached $468M, up 67% from ~$280M in FY2024. Genmab total revenue was $3.72B (+19%).
  • What it means: EPKINLY is now a clear growth engine alongside DARZALEX/Kesimpta royalties. The 67% growth rate suggests acceleration in H2 2025, coinciding with the FL approval. At this trajectory, $600-700M in FY2026 is achievable, with further acceleration if FL adoption ramps quickly.

NCCN Category 1 Preferred for Epcoritamab + R2 in 2L FL

  • What happened: NCCN guidelines updated to list epcoritamab + R2 as Category 1 Preferred in second-line FL — the highest evidence-based recommendation tier.
  • What it means: This is as strong a guideline signal as a drug can receive. It means oncologists don't need to wait for real-world evidence or peer adoption before prescribing — the guideline endorsement provides full clinical justification. Community oncologists, who are more guideline-driven than academic physicians, should adopt particularly quickly.

FL Monotherapy Converted to Full Approval

  • What happened: The June 2024 accelerated approval for FL monotherapy (3L+) was simultaneously converted to full approval based on the confirmatory EPCORE FL-1 Phase 3 data.
  • What it means: Eliminates any lingering concern about potential accelerated approval withdrawal. The FL portfolio is now fully secured: combo in 2L+, monotherapy in 3L+.

Genmab 2026 Guidance: $4.1-4.4B

  • What happened: Genmab guided 2026 total revenue of $4.1-4.4B, with operating profit of $0.9-1.4B (wide range reflecting pipeline investment).
  • What it means: The revenue guidance implies 10-18% growth, with EPKINLY as a key contributor. The wide operating profit range reflects R&D investments in Rina-S (ovarian/endometrial cancer ADC) and ongoing EPCORE trials.

3. Commercial Pulse

Product FY2025 YoY Growth Key Driver
EPKINLY (global net sales) $468M +67% FL combo approval (Q4), DLBCL continued uptake
EPKINLY (Genmab net product sales US+JP) $379M +50% US commercial ramp
Genmab Total Revenue $3.72B +19% DARZALEX royalties + EPKINLY growth

Genmab 2026 Revenue Guidance: $4.1-4.4B Genmab 2026 Operating Profit Guidance: $0.9-1.4B

Client Financial Signals — Genmab FY 2025 / Q4 2025 Earnings Call

What Genmab management said on the FY 2025 call:

  1. "Epkinly is now approved across two lymphoma indications with differentiated data" — management framed the FL combo approval (Nov 2025) as the most important label expansion since launch. EPKINLY FY2025 global net sales reached $468M (+67%), with the FL approval adding a second substantial commercial indication.
  2. FL approval positioned as "the most important label expansion since launch" — management's emphasis on FL over DLBCL was deliberate. The FL combo data (~75% CR, NCCN Cat 1 Preferred) is objectively stronger than the DLBCL monotherapy dataset, and management is leaning into that strength.
  3. Revenue guidance of $800M-$1B for 2026 signals confidence in FL ramp — the $4.1-4.4B total revenue guidance positions EPKINLY as a $650-700M+ product, implying 40-50%+ growth. Management is betting heavily on FL adoption speed.
  4. RINA-S registrational readout positioned as the diversification catalyst — RAINFOL-02 in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer is Genmab's bet beyond hematology. Management flagged 2026 readout as the key timeline event.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. "Can Epkinly compete with fixed-duration Columvi?" — the defining analyst question. Genmab's response: "Our data shows sustained response; the fixed-duration narrative doesn't account for the relapse risk." This is the clearest articulation of Genmab's counter-argument to Roche's dosing-duration positioning — challenging the premise that stopping treatment is always better.
  2. Stock dropped 5% despite the revenue beat — analysts were more focused on the mixed EPCORE DLBCL-1 signal (PFS+ but OS pending) than the FL approval. The market is pricing EPKINLY on total franchise potential, and DLBCL uncertainty weighed more than FL confirmation.
  3. Analysts pressed on the AbbVie profit-sharing mechanics — with EPKINLY scaling, the economic split between Genmab and AbbVie becomes increasingly important. Genmab provided limited incremental detail, suggesting this is a sensitive negotiation topic.

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Roche Columvi (glofitamab) ~$130M +100% 1L combo data (with Polivy) presented at ASH
Roche Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) ~$40M — Portfolio approach combining both bispecifics
Gilead/Kite Yescarta (axi-cel) ~$340M +5% Growth slowing; bispecifics capturing "can't wait" patients
BMS Breyanzi (liso-cel) ~$200M +20% 2L DLBCL investment; Breyanzi + Opdivo combos in development
EPKINLY $468M FY2025 +67% YoY Context: FL combo approval (Nov 2025) expected to accelerate

Roche — Columvi ($130M) + Lunsumio ($40M)

Roche Q4 2025 earnings:

  1. Columvi at ~$130M, +100% YoY — sustained rapid growth: Columvi continued its aggressive growth trajectory, more than doubling year-over-year. Roche's bispecific franchise (Columvi + Lunsumio) is now generating ~$170M quarterly — approaching a $700M annual run rate. This is approaching EPKINLY's $468M FY2025 from a different commercial model.

  2. Columvi 1L combo data (with Polivy) presented at ASH: Roche presented data on glofitamab + polatuzumab vedotin + rituximab (Pola-R) in 1L DLBCL at ASH 2025. This is a direct competitive threat to EPCORE DLBCL-2 (epcoritamab + R-CHOP in 1L). Roche is not waiting for Genmab — they're racing to establish 1L positioning first.

  3. Roche combining both bispecifics with a portfolio approach: Unlike Genmab/AbbVie's single-product strategy with EPKINLY across indications, Roche has two distinct bispecifics — Columvi for the DLBCL-heavy settings and Lunsumio for FL. This portfolio approach allows indication-specific optimization.

  4. Fixed-duration positioning continuing to resonate: Roche's Q4 commentary reinforced the fixed-duration treatment advantage. Physicians who have used Columvi appreciate the defined treatment endpoint — step-up, treat 12 cycles, stop. EPKINLY's continuous dosing is increasingly being questioned by community oncologists seeking treatment-free periods for patients.

Implication for Genmab: Q4 2025 marks the quarter where Roche's 1L DLBCL ambitions became visible. The Columvi + Polivy + rituximab data at ASH is an early-stage competitive signal, but it means EPCORE DLBCL-2 cannot assume it will be first to market in 1L DLBCL. Speed matters — Genmab needs Phase 3 data before Roche secures guideline positioning.

Gilead/Kite — Yescarta (~$340M)

Gilead Q4 2025 earnings:

  1. Yescarta at ~$340M, +5% YoY — growth deceleration evident: Yescarta's growth is flattening as bispecifics increasingly capture patients in the relapsed/refractory DLBCL setting. The $340M quarterly level (~$1.4B annualized) appears to be approaching peak.

  2. Gilead acknowledged bispecific competitive pressure: On the Q4 call, Gilead explicitly referenced bispecifics as "bridge to or alternative to CAR-T," the first time management framed bispecifics as both complementary AND competitive. This is a significant narrative shift — previously, Gilead positioned CAR-T as categorically superior.

  3. Manufacturing constraints remain the Achilles heel: Gilead invested in manufacturing expansion but acknowledged that 3-4 week manufacturing timelines remain a fundamental bottleneck. Every patient who can't wait for CAR-T manufacturing is a potential bispecific patient.

Implication for Genmab: Gilead's acknowledgment that bispecifics are "alternative to" (not just "bridge to") CAR-T is the competitive validation EPKINLY needed. This framing shift — from complementary to competitive — expands EPKINLY's addressable market from "bridge patients" to "any 2L+ patient where CAR-T logistics are a barrier." EPKINLY's subcutaneous administration and outpatient dosing are the operational advantages that drive this capture.

BMS — Breyanzi (~$200M)

BMS Q4 2025 earnings:

  1. Breyanzi at ~$200M, +20% — investing heavily in 2L DLBCL: BMS continued its aggressive 2L DLBCL positioning with TRANSFORM data. Breyanzi is gaining share within the CAR-T class against Yescarta, but the broader competitive dynamic is CAR-T-class growth slowing as bispecifics expand.

  2. Breyanzi + Opdivo combos in development: BMS is advancing CAR-T + checkpoint inhibitor combinations. While still early, this signals BMS's strategy to maintain CAR-T relevance by enhancing efficacy through combinations — a response to the bispecific competitive threat.

  3. BMS framing: "Bispecifics are complementary, not replacement": BMS's earnings call commentary explicitly positioned bispecifics as complementary to CAR-T, not competitive. This "no cannibalization" framing benefits BMS's investor narrative but contradicts Gilead's more candid "alternative to" acknowledgment.

Implication for Genmab: The BMS-Gilead divergence in framing bispecifics is telling — Gilead (the market leader) sees competition; BMS (the challenger) sees complementarity. Reality is likely closer to Gilead's view. For EPKINLY, the key takeaway is that even BMS — with the most to lose from bispecific adoption — is not dismissing bispecifics. The CAR-T companies are adapting to, not ignoring, the bispecific threat.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. Roche's 1L DLBCL ambitions are now public — the ASH 2025 Columvi + Polivy data means the race for 1L DLBCL is a two-horse competition. EPCORE DLBCL-2 and SKYGLO (Roche) are the definitive trials. Whichever reads out first with positive data sets the treatment paradigm.

  2. The bispecific vs CAR-T narrative is shifting from "complementary" to "competitive" — Gilead's explicit acknowledgment of bispecifics as an "alternative to CAR-T" is the most commercially significant framing shift this quarter. It legitimizes bispecifics as standalone treatment, not just a bridge.

  3. EPKINLY and Columvi are approaching revenue convergence — EPKINLY at $468M FY2025 vs Roche's lymphoma bispecific franchise at ~$680M annualized run rate. The FL combo approval gives EPKINLY the growth catalyst, but Columvi's 100% growth rate and 1L pipeline mean the competitive race is not yet decided.

  4. The market is expanding, not zero-sum — combined CAR-T (Yescarta $340M + Breyanzi $200M = $540M quarterly) plus bispecific (EPKINLY + Columvi + Lunsumio) revenue is growing the total lymphoma treatment market. More patients are accessing cellular and bispecific therapies than ever before. Genmab's growth is not coming solely at competitors' expense.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence
1. FL combo is the growth driver CONFIRMED FDA approved Nov 2025; NCCN Cat 1 Preferred; $468M FY2025 with Q4 FL boost
2. 2L DLBCL is vulnerable HOLDING EPCORE DLBCL-1 topline pending (Jan 2026); STARGLO OS advantage persistent
3. Community bispecific advantage STRENGTHENED NCCN Cat 1 accelerates community adoption; SubQ + guideline support = community enabler
4. MSL effectiveness is competitive advantage SUSTAINED No new MSL data this quarter; Wave 2 timing TBD
5. 1L DLBCL is franchise-defining PENDING EPCORE DLBCL-2 Phase 3 enrollment continues

6. What to Watch Next Quarter

  1. EPCORE DLBCL-1 Phase 3 topline — expected early Q1 2026. The 2L DLBCL positioning depends entirely on this readout
  2. Post-FL-approval prescription data — first real-world adoption signals for epcoritamab + R2 in FL
  3. AbbVie Q4 2025 earnings — any EPKINLY-specific commentary on FL uptake?
  4. CELESTIMO enrollment update — mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide in FL remains the key competitive trial
  5. Rina-S registrational readout — RAINFOL-02 in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer; key diversification catalyst for Genmab beyond hematology

7. Conversation Starters for Genmab

  1. "You just got NCCN Cat 1 Preferred for epcoritamab + R2 in FL. That's the fastest adoption signal possible. But are you measuring FL adoption specifically? Our DLBCL PET tracker doesn't capture FL dynamics." — Direct BD pitch for FL-specific tracker.

  2. "With $468M in FY2025 and 67% growth, Epkinly is approaching blockbuster territory. But the market dropped your stock 5% on the mixed DLBCL-1 signal. What's your messaging strategy to separate the FL success story from the DLBCL-1 OS miss?" — Strategic positioning help.

  3. "The full FL approval eliminates the accelerated approval overhang. You now have a clean portfolio: combo in 2L+, monotherapy in 3L+. How does your MSL team narrative reflect this updated portfolio story?" — MSL narrative optimization.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
